description: RGB shows coastal urban area with forest-covered hillside and partial cloud cover; SWIR reveals a distinct dark gray-brown elongated scar cutting through the bright green hillside behind the city, consistent with a fresh landslide exposing bare soil.
labeler_notes: Scar feature in SWIR is most diagnostic — dark irregular strip in hillside vegetation, matches event-peak date.

description: RGB is predominantly open ocean with a small vegetated island at the left edge; SWIR confirms dark water and bright green island vegetation with no anomalous offshore signature.
labeler_notes: Frame is almost entirely open water; minimal land content, no hazard signal visible.

description: RGB shows the same coastal urban-hillside area cleaner of cloud two weeks post-peak; SWIR retains a dark gray-brown elongated feature in the hillside consistent with a persistent landslide scar.
labeler_notes: Scar appears slightly smaller/less saturated than 2/22 but still clearly distinct from surrounding healthy green in SWIR.

description: RGB is dominated by dark open water with a small green island at the left; SWIR shows black water and bright green island with no anomalous surface features.
labeler_notes: Tiny bright dots at RGB right edge appear to be boats or sensor noise, not a hazard signature.

description: Both RGB and SWIR show a completely white frame with no visible ground — entire scene is obscured by thick cloud cover.
labeler_notes: No usable information in either band; full cloud cover.

description: RGB is near-white with near-total cloud cover; SWIR shows white cloud with faint cyan patches and no ground surface visible.
labeler_notes: Both bands cloud-saturated; ground completely uninterpretable.

description: RGB shows a healthy coastal town and dense green vegetation with minor cloud; SWIR confirms healthy bright-green vegetation and normal magenta urban tones with no offshore oil-slick signature visible in the water.
labeler_notes: Capture is before event peak; no oil visible. Labeled MEDIUM for coastal urban/port interest value.

description: RGB shows uniform dense green tropical forest with a small coastal strip and minor cloud patches; SWIR confirms predominantly healthy bright-green vegetation with no anomalous tones or offshore slick.
labeler_notes: Interior forest tile, pre-event capture, no hazard signal in either band.

description: RGB shows coastal urban area with dark streaky surface bands visible on the ocean offshore, distinct from normal open water; SWIR corroborates with an unusually dark and texturally smooth offshore area consistent with an oil slick suppressing sea-surface reflectance.
labeler_notes: Event-peak date; offshore dark linear/patchy features in RGB match SWIR darkening — consistent with surface oil slick. Confidence moderate; some cloud interference at frame edges.

description: RGB shows fragmented cloud cover dominating the frame with only small forest patches visible; SWIR confirms heavy cloud with bright white/yellow blooms obscuring the majority of the scene.
labeler_notes: Majority cloud cover in both bands prevents reliable assessment of oil spill or other surface hazard.
